political.s: BP contributed $542,799 in political donations and spent $3.73 million on lobbying in 2024. The company has historically supported both parties but maintains significant influence in energy and environmental policy. PAC contributions have leaned slightly toward Republicans but overall appears bipartisan.

charity.s: BP Foundation has provided hundreds of millions in global philanthropic support since 1953, focusing on STEM education and disaster relief. The foundation delivered over $6.5 million in matching gifts in 2023 and supported more than 8,500 organizations. However, charitable giving decreased significantly when Alaska operations transitioned to employee-choice models.
charity.amt: $6.5 million+ annually (matching gifts; foundation over $296 million since 2006)
charity.focus: STEM education, disaster relief, humanitarian aid, employee matching

environment.s: BP has one of the worst safety and environmental records among major oil companies, with a history of major incidents including the 2010 Deepwater Horizon disaster (11 deaths, $60+ billion in penalties and settlements), 2005 Texas City refinery explosion (15 deaths), and systematic violations. In 2025, BP announced plans to significantly reduce renewable energy investment and increase oil/gas production by 20% following shareholder pressure.

labor.s: BP faces extensive OSHA violations and poor safety records. The company was issued record-breaking fines of $87.43 million in 2009 for 439 willful safety violations. More recent violations in 2022-2023 included failures to implement process safety management and adequately train workers, resulting in fatal worker injuries. Systemic safety deficiencies at all organizational levels have been documented repeatedly.

dei.s: BP has established diversity, equity, and inclusion programs including 13 employee business resource groups, mandatory racial equity training, and supplier diversity initiatives. The company released its first global DEI report in 2021 and maintains commitment to advancing representation and inclusion across multiple diversity strands including race, gender, disability, and LGBTQ+ inclusion.

execPay.s: BP's CEO received $3.16 million in total compensation in 2025. Average BP executive compensation is $236,236 annually while average employee salary ranges from $117,646 to $140,000. The CEO compensation includes 2.4% salary and 97.6% bonuses/stock, indicating significant performance-based pay structure.
